content: Web plan parsing should no longer support legacy `done: boolean` steps; use `status: "pending" | "in_progress" | "done"` only for plan payloads in the web client.

Current runtime tool-settings behavior is global-first: loadDiligentConfig ignores project-level tools overrides, and app-server TOOLS_LIST/TOOLS_SET report and persist global config via getGlobalConfigPath/writeGlobalToolsConfig.

When users report 'front refresh still stale, server restart fixes it', treat it as potential server-runtime stale state beyond simple entry-count/leaf comparisons. Add reconcile diagnostics that include requested thread id, runtime/session ids, session path, and memory/disk tail fingerprints to narrow mismatch quickly.
User prefers minimal logging scope with always-on output for synchronization debugging (instead of debug flag gating).

In core tools, ls should resolve relative input paths against the runtime cwd (like glob/grep) to avoid sub-agent path ambiguity. createLsTool now accepts cwd and defaults assembly passes createLsTool(cwd).
User chose strict absolute-only path policy for filesystem tools (not relative-to-cwd resolution).

Path policy decided: read and ls require absolute paths; grep allows relative/absolute resolved against cwd; apply_patch requires relative-only paths; write requires relative-only; glob requires absolute path (or absolute cwd default).
User prefers explicit confirmation before running local test commands; in this session they chose not to execute `bun run web:test` immediately.

Skills should be loaded via a dedicated skill tool path (not via read tool). Both slash invocation (/{skill}) and natural handling should flow through skill-tool semantics, with chat-visible tool items.

Web slash commands support skills via InitializeResponse: protocol schema has optional `skills: SkillInfo[]` field (name + description), web server maps from runtimeConfig.skills, web client merges them into slash command list via buildCommandList(). Skill invocation sends `/<skillname>` as regular TURN_START message — server-side parseSlashSkillInvocation in thread-handlers.ts handles the transformation to skill tool calls. No dedicated SKILLS_LIST RPC method needed.

For the planned Web slash-command/TUI parity work, the user decided: keep the Web slash autocomplete list, but remove submenu/dialog behavior; command click/Enter should execute immediately; arg-required commands selected without args should show usage; remove web-only `/mode` and `/effort`; exclude dialog-dependent or browser-ambiguous TUI commands from Web slash support, including `/provider`, `/tools`, and `/delete`.

When users report 'front refresh still stale, server restart fixes it', treat it as potential server-runtime stale state beyond simple entry-count/leaf comparisons. Add reconcile diagnostics that include requested thread id, runtime/session ids, session path, and memory/disk tail fingerprints to narrow mismatch quickly.

User decided model and effort changes must be isolated per thread/session, must not affect other threads or any currently running loop, and should apply starting from the next user turn only. Thread-specific model should be persisted in session history and restored on resume. Turn-ending errors should be logged with the reason and persisted as timeline items visible in the Web UI after refresh.

The `write` and `apply_patch` tools may silently fail or write to wrong paths when the server's process.cwd() differs from the thread's cwd. This is because the web server process runs from packages/web (process.cwd() = packages/web), but tools resolve relative paths against the thread cwd. Always use `bash` with absolute paths (e.g., `cat > /absolute/path`) as a fallback when write/apply_patch fails silently. The root cause was bash.ts using process.cwd() instead of the thread cwd - fixed by converting to createBashTool(cwd) factory pattern.

D093 (fine-grained streaming delta types) cancelled. The existing item/delta union with type discriminator (messageText/messageThinking/toolOutput) is sufficient because: 1) clients already know tool type from item/started's toolName field, 2) tool_end carries ToolRenderPayload for render-type distinction, 3) splitting method names just moves the branch from delta.type to method name with no structural benefit.

Model-dependent tool loading: OpenAI models get `apply_patch` + relative-path `write`; all other providers (anthropic, gemini, etc.) get `edit` + `multi_edit` + absolute-path `write`. The `buildDefaultTools()` function accepts an optional `provider` parameter. The factory.ts passes `resolvedModel.provider` to buildDefaultTools. Edit tools are in packages/core/src/tools/edit.ts, absolute-path write variant is `createWriteAbsoluteTool()` in write.ts.

Model-dependent tool loading: OpenAI models get `apply_patch` only (no `write` — apply_patch handles both creation and modification); all other providers (anthropic, gemini, etc.) get `edit` + `multi_edit` + absolute-path `write`.

Config and system folder layout (finalized):
- Global config: ~/.diligent/config.jsonc
- Global auth: ~/.diligent/auth.jsonc
- Project config: .diligent/config.jsonc
- Global skills: ~/.diligent/skills/
- Input history: ~/.diligent/history
- Auth not configurable at project level
- Old paths (~/.config/diligent/diligent.jsonc, ~/.config/diligent/auth.json, .diligent/diligent.jsonc) no longer exist

bun test crashes in this agent's bash tool environment on Windows with "panic: invalid enum value". Root cause: Bun's test runner crashes on Windows when stdout/stderr is a pipe (not a real console handle). This affects both Bun v1.3.10 and v1.3.11-canary. All reporters (default, dots, junit) crash identically. The fix is impossible from within the agent — user must run `bun run test` from their own terminal (PowerShell/cmd). No workarounds exist: TERM=dumb, NO_COLOR, --reporter flags, new console windows, all fail.

Diligent global plugin auto-discovery: placing a plugin package under ~/.diligent/plugins/<packageName> is sufficient for it to be loaded automatically via discoverGlobalPlugins(). No explicit entry in .diligent/config.jsonc tools.plugins array is needed. The catalog.ts auto-discovery merges discovered plugins with explicitly configured ones.

systemPromptFile config field added: config.jsonc supports `systemPromptFile` (string path) to load system prompt from an external file. Priority: systemPrompt (inline string) > systemPromptFile (file path) > default template. File paths can be absolute or relative to cwd. Template variables {{currentDate}}, {{cwd}}, {{platform}} are substituted in file-loaded prompts. Schema in packages/core/src/config/schema.ts, loading logic in packages/core/src/config/runtime.ts.

OVERDARE Studio instance tools can add Script and Part objects, but current toolset cannot create or reposition SpawnLocation directly. To move spawn behavior, use a server Script in ServerScriptService that teleports characters on CharacterAdded.

OVERDARE packaging pipeline decisions:
1. thirdparty/overdare/ has a workspace structure: plugins/ subdirectory for plugins, docs/ for documentation, config.jsonc + systemprompt.txt at root
2. Package command: `bun run package --version X.Y.Z --platforms windows-x64 --package thirdparty/overdare`
3. System prompt injection: During packaging, systemprompt.txt from --package dir replaces packages/core/src/agent/templates/system-prompt.txt temporarily (inject before sidecar build, restore in finally block). NOT via config.jsonc systemPromptFile.
4. assembleDefaults handles: .jsonc files from root → defaults/, plugins/ subdirs → bun build → bundled index.js, other dirs (docs/) → plain copy to defaults/, skips node_modules
5. init.rs deploys ALL files and dirs from bundled defaults/ to ~/.diligent/ (not just .jsonc). Files only on first run (!dest.exists()), dirs always synced.
6. tauri.conf.json maps entire resources/defaults directory, not individual files.
7. --package path is resolved relative to ROOT, not process.cwd().

For plugin-defined tool render payloads, the user decided plugins do not need to statically import or share the exact render-type definitions. Plugins may return ad-hoc render payload objects, and validation can happen at runtime instead of compile-time schema coupling.

Manual thread compaction originally did not mark runtime.isRunning or emit thread/status/changed busy/idle around compactNow(). As a result, Web UI could not reliably show an in-progress state for manual compaction. Fix: wrap handleThreadCompactStart with runtime.isRunning=true/false and emit THREAD_STATUS_CHANGED busy before compactNow() and idle in finally after completion.

External plugin deployment works by placing a package directory under ~/.diligent/plugins/<packageName>. For thirdparty/examples/external-tool-plugin, a built package with package.json main/exports -> ./dist/index.js loaded successfully from ~/.diligent/plugins/example-tool-plugin, and createTools().execute() returned a valid ToolRenderPayload object.
User wants custom tools/plugins to support an explicit parallel execution option, similar to built-in tools with supportParallel.
Desktop app sidecar now defaults web server logs to ~/.diligent/logs/YYYYMMDD-PID.log by passing --log-file to the bundled web server on startup.

packages/core cleanup review found stale naming drift: src/config/instructions.ts only discovers AGENTS.md, but tests in packages/core/test/config-instructions.test.ts and instructions-knowledge.test.ts still refer to CLAUDE.md in descriptions/fixtures/expected paths. There is also a deprecated bashTool singleton export in src/tools/bash.ts still used by tests; preferred API is createBashTool(cwd).
